source: CMS Medicaid & CHIP Core Set Quality Measures
note: 2024 Core Set (services primarily CY2023). Rates used in calculating state mean/median only.

FUM-CH.name: Follow-Up After Emergency Department Visit for Mental Illness: Ages 6 to 17
FUM-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
FUM-CH.rate_def: Percentage of Emergency Department (ED) Visits for Mental Illness or Intentional Self-Harm with a Follow-Up Visit Within

FUA-CH.name: Follow-Up After Emergency Department Visit for Substance Use: Ages 13 to 17
FUA-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
FUA-CH.rate_def: Percentage of Emergency Department (ED) Visits for Substance Use Disorder (SUD) or Drug Overdose with a Follow-Up Visit

FUH-CH.name: Follow-Up After Hospitalization for Mental Illness: Ages 6 to 17
FUH-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
FUH-CH.rate_def: Percentage of Discharges for Children Hospitalized for Treatment of Mental Illness or Intentional Self-Harm with a Follo

ADD-CH.name: Follow-Up Care for Children Prescribed Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) Medication: Ages 6 to 12
ADD-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
ADD-CH.rate_def: Percentage Newly Prescribed ADHD Medication with at Least One Follow-Up Visit During the 30-Day Initiation Phase: Ages 6

APM-CH.name: Metabolic Monitoring for Children and Adolescents on Antipsychotics: Ages 1 to 17
APM-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
APM-CH.rate_def: Percentage with Two or More Antipsychotic Prescriptions that had Metabolic Testing for Blood Glucose: Ages 1 to 11

APP-CH.name: Use of First-Line Psychosocial Care for Children and Adolescents on Antipsychotics: Ages 1 to 17
APP-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
APP-CH.rate_def: Percentage who had a New Prescription for an Antipsychotic Medication and had Documentation of Psychosocial Care as Firs

AMB-CH.name: Ambulatory Care: Emergency Department (ED) Visits: Ages 0 to 19
AMB-CH.domain: Care of Acute and Chronic Conditions
AMB-CH.type: Lower rates are better for this measure
AMB-CH.rate_def: Emergency Department Visits per 1,000 Beneficiary Months: Ages 0 to 19

AMR-CH.name: Asthma Medication Ratio: Ages 5 to 18
AMR-CH.domain: Care of Acute and Chronic Conditions
AMR-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
AMR-CH.rate_def: Percentage with Persistent Asthma who had a Ratio of Controller Medications to Total Asthma Medications of 0.50 or Great

AAB-CH.name: Avoidance of Antibiotic Treatment for Acute Bronchitis/Bronchiolitis: Ages 3 Months to 17 Years
AAB-CH.domain: Care of Acute and Chronic Conditions
AAB-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
AAB-CH.rate_def: Percentage of Episodes for Beneficiaries with a Diagnosis of Acute Bronchitis/Bronchiolitis that did not Result in an An

OEV-CH.name: Oral Evaluation, Dental Services: Ages <1 through 20
OEV-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
OEV-CH.rate_def: Percentage of Enrolled Children Under Age 21 Who Received a Comprehensive or Periodic Oral Evaluation: Ages <1 through 2

SFM-CH.name: Sealant Receipt on Permanent First Molars: Age 10
SFM-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
SFM-CH.rate_def: Percentage who Received a Sealant on at Least One Permanent First Molar Tooth by their 10th Birthday

TFL-CH.name: Topical Fluoride for Children: Ages 1 through 20
TFL-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
TFL-CH.rate_def: Percentage of Enrolled Children who Received at Least Two Topical Fluoride Applications as Dental or Oral Health Service

CPC-CH.name: CAHPS Health Plan Survey 5.1H - Child Version Including Medicaid and Children with Chronic Conditions Supplemental Items: Ages 0 to 17
CPC-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
CPC-CH.rate_def: Percentage whose Caregiver Selected They "Always" Got Needed Care, on a Four-Point Scale Ranging from "Never" to "Always

CCW-CH.name: Contraceptive Care - All Women Ages 15 to 20
CCW-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
CCW-CH.rate_def: Percentage of All Women at Risk of Unintended Pregnancy Provided a Most Effective or Moderately Effective Method of Cont

LBW-CH.name: Live Births Weighing Less Than 2,500 Grams
LBW-CH.type: Lower rates are better for this measure
LBW-CH.rate_def: Percentage of Live Births Weighing Less Than 2,500 Grams

LRCD-CH.name: Low-Risk Cesarean Delivery
LRCD-CH.type: Lower rates are better for this measure
LRCD-CH.rate_def: Percentage of Nulliparous, Term, Singleton, in a Cephalic Presentation Births Delivered by Cesarean

PPC2-CH.name: Prenatal and Postpartum Care: Under Age 21
PPC2-CH.type: Higher rates are better for this measure
PPC2-CH.rate_def: Percentage of Women Under Age 21 Delivering a Live Birth with a Prenatal Care Visit in the First Trimester, on or before

WCV-CH.domain: Primary Care Access and Preventive Care

WCV-CH.rate_def: Percentage with at Least 1 Well-Care Visit with a Primary Care Practitioner or Obstetrician/Gynecologist: Ages 3 to 11
